summary: PR 2519 linked `melee/lb/lbarchive.c` by changing it from Equivalent to Matching, inlining `lbArchive_LoadArchive` inside two archive-loading varargs wrappers, and adjusting lbarchive-adjacent string symbol metadata in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The bot report credited two new matches, `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC`, plus linked-code and linked-data gains.

key_files.role: Primary source change: two functions replaced calls to `lbArchive_LoadArchive(filename)` with a locally inlined allocation/load/initialize sequence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c hunks for lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC

key_files.role: Build/matching classification changed `melee/lb/lbarchive.c` from Equivalent to Matching.
Symbol metadata adjusted around lbFile/lbDvd/lbHeap string data, mostly changing generated string labels from global or unspecified scope to local and adding a local data label.

what_changed.text: `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC` each gained `tmp` and `archive2` locals and inline the `lbArchive_LoadArchive` sequence when no preloaded archive is found.

The inlined sequence allocates file data with `lbHeap_80015BD0(0, OSRoundUp32B(lbFile_800163D8(filename)))`, stores it in `tmp`, allocates `sizeof(HSD_Archive)`, loads the file with `lbFile_8001668C`, initializes via `lbArchive_InitializeDAT`, and assigns `archive = archive2`.
`symbols.txt` changed several nearby generated string labels from global or unspecified scope to local, renamed `@126` to `@120`, and added local label `...data.0` at `.data:0x803BA588`.
The decomp bot reported +768 matched code bytes, +2664 linked code bytes, +192 linked data bytes, and two new matches for `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC`.

smart_moves.text: Kept the source change narrow: only the two near-matching lbarchive wrapper functions were altered, with a matching-status update and minimal symbol metadata changes.
Used explicit source comments `// Inlined lbArchive_LoadArchive` at both replacement sites, making the matching tactic easy for reviewers and future decompilers to identify.
Preserved address-style function names such as `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC` rather than inventing semantics not shown in the PR evidence.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small wrapper functions that are already near 100%, an explicit inline expansion of a helper call can be a valid narrow matching tactic when verifier output names the exact functions that become matched.
decomp_lessons.caveat: This is not evidence that helper inlining should be used broadly; loaded standards still require explicit build/objdiff/checkdiff evidence and narrow scope for manual inline-style tactics.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Linking a translation unit may require matching source edits plus symbol metadata cleanup in the same PR, but the evidence should distinguish text-section function closure from data/symbol metadata changes.
decomp_lessons.caveat: The PR does not explain why each symbol scope change was necessary, so broader data-ownership rules should not be inferred.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style function names were retained for the two matched functions: `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC`.
Temporary locals introduced for matching used generic names `tmp` and `archive2`; the PR does not provide semantic naming rationale.
Generated data labels in `symbols.txt` stayed address/number-style, with scope adjustments rather than semantic renames.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Manual inline expansion of `lbArchive_LoadArchive` in `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The previous single helper call was replaced by explicit file-size lookup, rounded heap allocation, archive allocation, file read, DAT initialization, and assignment to `archive`.
verification_evidence: decomp-dev[bot] reported both functions moved to 100.00% and listed them as two new matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced otherwise-unused-looking padding locals `u32 pad; u32 pad2;` inside the inline blocks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR diff shows these locals in both inlined blocks, but does not explain their stack/codegen purpose.
verification_evidence: Bot report names the two functions as newly matched.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjusted string symbol scopes to local around lbarchive/lbdvd/lbheap data while linking the object.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Several `@NN` labels changed from `scope:global` or unspecified scope to `scope:local`; one local `...data.0` label was added.
verification_evidence: Bot report included +192 linked data bytes, but the PR evidence does not tie each symbol edit to a specific data diff.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.text: Approved with no review body or inline review comments in the loaded slice.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported two new matches and measurable matched/linked code/data gains for GALE01.

follow_up_queries: symbols.txt lbarchive string labels scope local
manual inline helper call pad pad2 decomp

evidence_refs: metadata_json for PR identity, changed-file counts, merge state
notes: PR body is empty, so author rationale is not available.
No inline review comments are present in the loaded slice.
Verifier evidence is from the decomp bot report, not a manually included objdiff transcript.
The PR evidence supports that the two listed functions matched, but it does not explain the necessity of each individual symbol scope edit or padding local.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow helper-call inlining closed two lbarchive wrapper matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2519, replacing `lbArchive_LoadArchive(filename)` with an explicit inline allocation/load/initialize sequence inside `lbArchive_80017040` and `lbArchive_800171CC` coincided with the bot reporting both functions as new 100.00% matches. Treat this as a narrow, evidence-backed tactic, not a general preference for manual inlining.

source_update_candidates.title: `melee/lb/lbarchive.c` linked as Matching in PR 2519
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2519 changed `configure.py` so `melee/lb/lbarchive.c` is configured as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(Equivalent, ...)`; the bot reported two new lbarchive matches.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-level matching status is source-owned and should be curated as a path fact rather than promoted directly by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: lbarchive-adjacent string labels made local in PR 2519
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2519 changed several generated string symbols near `.data:0x803BA588` and `.sdata:0x804D37C0` from global or unspecified scope to local and added local label `...data.0` at `.data:0x803BA588`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol scope and label ownership belong in curated source/path metadata; the PR slice does not justify a global data-standard update.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a general rule that `lbArchive_LoadArchive` should always be manually inlined; the evidence only covers two wrapper functions in this PR.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond an empty approval.
Do not promote the `pad` and `pad2` locals as normal style; the PR gives no rationale beyond the resulting bot-reported matches.
Do not infer broad data ownership rules from the symbol scope changes without additional section/split evidence.
